title: G2: Multiplicativity of the signature under products tau(MxN)=tau(M)tau(N)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Claim to prove.** For closed oriented $M^{4k},N^{4l}$, $\tau(M\times N)=\tau(M)\tau(N)$.

**Required argument.** Decompose $H^{2(k+l)}(M\times N;\mathbb{R})=\bigoplus_{a+b=2(k+l)}H^a(M)\otimes H^b(N)$ (Künneth). The intersection form splits into: (i) the 'diagonal' part $H^{2k}(M)\otimes H^{2l}(N)$ carrying the **tensor product** of the two middle forms, with signature $\mathrm{sgn}(A\otimes B)=\mathrm{sgn}(A)\,\mathrm{sgn}(B)$ for nondegenerate symmetric $A,B$; (ii) off-diagonal blocks pairing $H^a(M)\otimes H^b(N)$ with $H^{2k-a}(M)\otimes H^{2l-b}(N)$ which are **hyperbolic** (signature 0). 

**What must be shown rigorously:** the cup-product signs $(-1)^{ab}$ make each off-diagonal block a hyperbolic (maximal-isotropic) summand contributing 0, and the diagonal tensor-product identity $\mathrm{sgn}(A\otimes B)=\mathrm{sgn}(A)\mathrm{sgn}(B)$. Sign bookkeeping is the classic source of error.
